service_description: API for fetching npm package bundle sizes, version history, tree-shaking metadata, per-export costs, and similar package suggestions from Bundlephobia.

tools.name: get_package_size
tools.description: Get the minified and gzipped bundle size for an npm package. Returns size in bytes, gzip size, resolved version, description, and repository URL. Use this when you need to know how much a package adds to a JavaScript bundle.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The npm package name, optionally pinned with @ syntax (e.g., 'react', 'react@18.2.0', '@scope/package@1.0.0'). Omit the version to get the latest.

tools.name: check_tree_shaking
tools.description: Check whether an npm package supports tree-shaking and side-effect-free imports. Returns hasJSModule (whether an ES module entry exists), hasSideEffects (false means safe to tree-shake), and hasJSNext flags. Use this when deciding whether named imports like 'import { format } from date-fns' will be bundled efficiently.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The npm package name, optionally pinned with @ syntax (e.g., 'lodash-es', 'date-fns', 'lodash@4.17.21').

tools.name: get_package_history
tools.description: Fetch historical bundle sizes for multiple versions of an npm package. Returns an object keyed by version string, each containing size, gzip, and dependency data. Use this to track how bundle size has changed across releases or to compare the cost of upgrading.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of historical versions to return. Defaults to 15 if omitted.

tools.name: get_recent_packages
tools.description: Get recently searched npm packages on Bundlephobia, including their name, version, search count, and last searched timestamp. Use this to discover trending or frequently evaluated packages.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent packages to return.

tools.name: get_similar_packages
tools.description: Find similar or alternative npm packages to a given package. Returns a category label (e.g., 'HTTP client libraries for Node.js') and a list of similar package names. Use this when evaluating lighter-weight alternatives or when a package is too large.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The npm package name to find alternatives for (e.g., 'axios', 'moment', 'lodash').

tools.name: get_exports_sizes
tools.description: Get the individual bundle size for each named export of a tree-shakeable npm package. Returns a list of exports sorted by size, each with name, minified size, and gzip size in bytes. Use this to find the cheapest specific function to import, or to compare the cost of individual utilities (e.g., lodash-es/pick vs lodash-es/merge). Only works for packages that expose named ES module exports.

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The npm package name, optionally pinned with @ syntax (e.g., 'lodash-es', 'date-fns', 'rxjs'). Best used with packages known to support tree-shaking.
